skill.name: test-driven-development
skill.description: Guides agents through strict test-driven development for features, bug fixes, refactoring, and behavior changes.
skill.summary: Enforces red-green-refactor workflow before implementation code is written.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Human review found these are false positives from Markdown code fences, test examples, diagrams, prose, and references to local test commands. No prompt injection, data exfiltration, network access, filesystem mutation, credential access, or executable install scripts were found.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The reported external command detections are code fences, TypeScript test snippets, a DOT diagram, and npm test examples. They are documentation examples, not executable skill code or shell invocation logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are Markdown blocks and illustrative test examples. There are no scripts, manifests, or runtime files that execute these snippets.

low_findings.title: Static blocker patterns are contextual false positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography, system reconnaissance, and network reconnaissance patterns were not supported by semantic review. The files discuss testing behavior and mocks, with no encryption use, host probing, network calls, or data exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed files contain only Markdown guidance and example tests. Searches for network, credential, execution, and prompt-injection indicators found no corroborating malicious behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams lose confidence when tests are written after implementation. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through red-green-refactor work before production code changes.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to write a failing test before implementation.
Defines red, green, and refactor checkpoints for code changes.
Explains how to verify expected test failure before coding.
Provides examples of strong and weak tests.
Identifies common testing anti-patterns around mocks and test-only APIs.
Supplies a completion checklist for test-first development.
limitations: It provides process guidance, not an automated test runner.
It does not choose a test framework for a project.
It may be too strict for throwaway prototypes or generated code.
It requires the agent to inspect the existing repository and test commands.

use_cases.title: Add a feature with test coverage
use_cases.description: Use the skill to define expected behavior, write the first failing test, implement only what passes, and refactor after green.

use_cases.title: Fix a regression safely
use_cases.description: Use the skill to reproduce the bug with a failing test before changing production code.

use_cases.title: Review test design decisions
use_cases.description: Use the anti-pattern guidance to avoid tests that only verify mocks, partial fixtures, or test-only production methods.

prompt_templates.title: Start a simple TDD change
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Help me add this behavior with a failing test first, then minimal implementation, then refactor.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a bug with a regression test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Reproduce this bug with a failing test, confirm the failure reason, then implement the smallest fix.

prompt_templates.title: Refactor without behavior drift
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development skill. Identify the behavior that must stay stable, add or confirm tests, then refactor while keeping tests green.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a test strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the test-driven-development and testing anti-pattern guidance. Review my planned tests for mock overuse, missing red checks, and weak assertions.

output_examples.input: Add retry behavior to a service call.
output: A proposed first failing test that describes retry behavior.
A check that the failure is caused by missing retry logic.
A minimal implementation plan after the red step.
A refactor checkpoint after all tests pass.

output_examples.input: Fix empty email submission being accepted.
output: A regression test that expects an email required error.
A red-step verification note for the current failure.
A minimal validation change after the failing test exists.
A final checklist for passing tests and clean output.

output_examples.input: Review tests that depend on mocks.
output: A list of assertions that test mock behavior instead of product behavior.
Suggested lower-level mocks or real components.
A revised test intent focused on observable behavior.

best_practices: Write one focused failing test before any production code.
Verify the test fails for the expected reason before implementing.
Refactor only after the new and existing tests pass.
anti_patterns: Writing production code first and adding tests afterward.
Asserting that mocks exist instead of checking real behavior.
Adding production methods that exist only for test cleanup.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to run the project test command and verify red and green states.

faq.question: Can I use it with any programming language?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is language independent, but examples use TypeScript and npm test commands.

faq.question: Does it require a specific test framework?
faq.answer: No. It expects the agent to use the test framework already present in the repository.

faq.question: When should I skip this skill?
faq.answer: Skip only for throwaway prototypes, generated code, or configuration changes after human approval.

faq.question: How does it handle mocks?
faq.answer: It discourages testing mock behavior and recommends mocking only when the dependency role is understood.

faq.question: Is this useful for bug fixes?
faq.answer: Yes. It asks for a failing regression test before the fix, then verifies the fix keeps tests green.
